KPR Charities established the KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology in the year 2009 as a new generation Engineering college in Coimbatore. The institute is affiliated with Anna University with the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) approval, and it is committed to an incomparable learning experience. The institute is committed to being a universally recognized institute in Engineering and Technology by the year 2020. The institute is located in the beautiful City Coimbatore on a rambling campus of 150 Acres of Land.

* The institute’s vision is to develop into a leading institute of academic brilliance by conveying technological, rational, and specialized skills to the learners for meeting the assorted essentials of the society, nation, industry, and the domain at large.

* The institute is dedicated to delivering significantly based education and improving practical abilities and nonstop valuation of learning and teaching procedure over scholarly activities.

* It improves exploration and pioneering activities in cooperation with the institute and industry of repute and guarantees academic progression to sustain ethics, culture, and communal responsibility.

The institutes take great pride in the main feature such as top-class infrastructure, top-flight faculty, high pass percentage, unique students’ project, well-stacked library, and excellent placement record, etc. Its quality policy is to convey education to fetch academic superiority, make sure students maintain ethical and moral standards, provide the mandate-driven essentials of numerous stakeholders, encourage research, and assist technology transmission of societal consequences.

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## History
### Establishment
The KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) was established in 2009 by the KPR Group, a diversified conglomerate founded in 1984 with roots in the textile industry. Under the leadership of Dr. K. P. Ramasamy, popularly known as KPR, who began his entrepreneurial journey in 1971 as a power loom cloth manufacturer before expanding into manufacturing, engineering, and philanthropy, the institute marked the group's entry into higher education. Dr. Ramasamy's transition from industrial ventures to education was driven by a commitment to fostering innovation and skill development, positioning KPRIET as a key pillar in the group's educational initiatives.
The institute was strategically located in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, a renowned industrial hub often called the "Manchester of South India" due to its concentration of engineering, textile, and manufacturing sectors, which provided an ideal ecosystem for industry-aligned engineering education. This choice enabled strong ties with local industries from the outset, enhancing practical training opportunities for students. Affiliated with Anna University, Chennai, from its inception, KPRIET was approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) to offer undergraduate programs, ensuring compliance with national standards.
The initial vision of KPRIET centered on delivering quality engineering education emphasizing practical skills, innovative teaching, research, and industry relevance, with the goal of producing engineers equipped to address real-world challenges. Operations commenced on a greenfield campus spanning 66 acres in Coimbatore's suburbs, featuring basic infrastructure such as foundational classrooms, laboratories, and facilities tailored for core undergraduate disciplines including mechanical, electronics, and computer science engineering. This setup supported early student intake focused on hands-on learning, laying the foundation for the institute's growth as a center for technical excellence

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## History
### Growth and Milestones
Since its establishment in 2009, KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology has undergone significant expansion in its academic offerings. Postgraduate programs were introduced to complement the initial undergraduate courses, allowing for advanced studies in engineering disciplines. The institute had expanded further to include PhD programs, establishing research centers in select departments under Anna University affiliation to foster doctoral-level research and innovation.
A pivotal milestone came in 2019 when the University Grants Commission (UGC) granted the institute full autonomous status, enabling greater flexibility in curriculum design, examination processes, and industry-aligned program development. The institute achieved NAAC accreditation with an A grade in 2015, reflecting its early commitment to quality education, followed by re-accreditation in 2019 with a CGPA of 3.08. Starting in 2017, the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) accredited select undergraduate programs such as Civil, Computer Science, Electronics and Communication, Electrical and Electronics, and Mechanical Engineering, with renewals extending up to 2026 for several of these. Additionally, KPRIET describes itself as a "21st Century Engineering College" focusing on modern pedagogy and employability skills.
The institute's student body has grown substantially from an initial enrollment of approximately 500 in its founding year to over 3,500 students by 2025, supported by a faculty strength that increased to 260 members, many with PhD qualifications. This expansion underscores the institute's rising appeal and capacity to deliver quality engineering education. In 2015, KPRIET established the KPR Incubation Centre (KIC) to nurture startups and entrepreneurship, providing mentorship, funding support, and facilities for student-led innovations in ICT and manufacturing sectors. Complementing this, the institute forged strategic partnerships with industries and international universities for curriculum enhancement, including joint research projects and skill development programs to align education with global standards. In 2023, NAAC extended the accreditation validity period, and in the NIRF 2025 rankings, KPRIET was placed in the 101-150 band for engineering institutions

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is a private autonomous engineering college established in 2009 and located in the suburbs of Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India. Affiliated to Anna University, Chennai, and approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), it serves approximately 3,500 students with a faculty of 260 members, emphasizing innovation, research, and industry collaboration. The institute is accredited by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) with a CGPA of 3.08 on a four-point scale, earning an 'A' grade in its first cycle, valid until December 31, 2024. Several undergraduate programs, including those in Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Civil Engineering, are accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) for periods extending up to 2026.
KPRIET offers a range of undergraduate programs such as B.E. in Biomedical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Computer Science and Engineering (Cyber Security), Computer Science and Engineering (Data Science), Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning), Information Technology; postgraduate programs including M.E. in Applied Electronics, CAD/CAM, Computer Science and Engineering, Communication Systems, Construction Engineering and Management, Embedded Systems, Power Electronics and Drives, Structural Engineering, Thermal Engineering, VLSI Design, and M.Tech in Information Technology, as well as M.B.A. and Ph.D. programs across engineering disciplines. The curriculum integrates challenge-based learning, value-added courses, and industry-oriented electives to foster skills like creativity, critical thinking, collaboration, and communication, aligning with 21st-century engineering needs.
In the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2025, KPRIET is placed in the 101-150 rank band for Engineering institutions in India. The institute maintains 65 research laboratories, promotes joint projects with industries and universities, and supports initiatives in entrepreneurship, sustainable development, and corporate social responsibility through centers like the Centre for Research and Development and the Innovation and Incubation Centre. However, in 2025, a Nature report noted the institute among those with a high number of retracted academic publications. Its campus features modern facilities including smart classrooms, a virtual tour-enabled environment, hostels, sports amenities, and green energy practices, contributing to a holistic educational experience. KPRIET also boasts strong placement outcomes, with graduates recruited by leading companies, and an active alumni network that aids in career development and institutional growth

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology - KPRIET Coimbatore Admissions 2026
### About KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
Affiliated to Anna University, the KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology is located in Coimbatore. Since its inception in 2009, it has been providing innovative education with the help of modern teaching methods, significant research, and industry interaction. Approved by AICTE, the institute is accredited by NBA and NAAC with A Grade and is recognised by Department and Scientific and Industrial Research.

The vision of the institute is to work towards creating a positive environment at the institute so that young minds can make a difference in the society. It also aims to make the students competent so they can offer innovative solutions to modern problems.

The institute has 12 departments and cutting-edge research centres along with 60+ laboratories. The institute offers 12 UG and 4 PG courses including:

* **B.E**. in Biomedical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering (AI & ML), Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, and Mechatronics Engineering

* **B.Tech **in** **Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Computer Science & Business systems, Information Technology

* **M.E. **in CAD/ CAM, Computer Science and Engineering, Structural Engineering, Chemical Engineering, and VLSI Design

* Research programs are also available at the institute

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## Academics
### Departments and Courses
KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology operates 12 academic departments, encompassing core engineering disciplines, emerging technologies, management, and foundational sciences. These include the Departments of Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Information Technology, Chemical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Mechatronics Engineering, Management Studies, and Science and Humanities. Each department focuses on specialized curricula aligned with industry needs and research opportunities, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration across fields like AI integration in traditional engineering and sustainable practices in chemical and civil domains.
The institute offers undergraduate programs leading to B.E. or B.Tech degrees in 13 specializations, each spanning four years and designed to build foundational and advanced technical skills. These programs include Civil Engineering (60 seats), Mechanical Engineering (60 seats), Electrical and Electronics Engineering (60 seats), Electronics and Communication Engineering (240 seats), Computer Science and Engineering (300 seats), Information Technology (180 seats), Chemical Engineering (60 seats), Biomedical Engineering (60 seats), Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (180 seats), Mechatronics Engineering (60 seats), Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (120 seats), Computer Science and Engineering (Cyber Security) (60 seats), and Computer Science and Business Systems (60 seats). Intake capacities vary by specialization, typically ranging from 60 to 300 seats to accommodate diverse student interests while maintaining quality education.
At the postgraduate level, KPRIET provides M.E. and M.Tech programs in five specializations, each lasting two years and emphasizing advanced research and application: Computer-Aided Design/Computer-Aided Manufacturing (CAD/CAM), Computer Science and Engineering, Data Sciences, Structural Engineering, and VLSI Design. Additionally, a two-year Master of Business Administration (MBA) program is offered through the Department of Management Studies, focusing on business acumen integrated with technical knowledge. The institute also supports PhD programs in select fields, including Biomedical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, Civil Engineering, and Computer Science Engineering, with a research-oriented structure that encourages original contributions and industry partnerships.
As an autonomous institution, KPRIET's curricula incorporate flexible syllabi updated to reflect post-2019 advancements, featuring departmental and open electives for customization, industry-integrated projects in the final semester, and interdisciplinary options such as AI applications across engineering streams. These elements promote practical learning through value-added certificate courses, entrepreneurship tracks, and collaborations with industries for real-world problem-solving

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
Ara    Eve    Leo    Sal

1x

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is a private autonomous engineering college established in 2009 and located in the suburbs of Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India. Affiliated to Anna University, Chennai, and approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), it serves approximately 3,500 students with a faculty of 260 members, emphasizing innovation, research, and industry collaboration. The institute is accredited by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) with a CGPA of 3.08 on a four-point scale, earning an 'A' grade in its first cycle, valid until December 31, 2024. Several undergraduate programs, including those in Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Civil Engineering, are accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) for periods extending up to 2026.
KPRIET offers a range of undergraduate programs such as B.E. in Biomedical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Computer Science and Engineering (Cyber Security), Computer Science and Engineering (Data Science), Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning), Information Technology; postgraduate programs including M.E. in Applied Electronics, CAD/CAM, Computer Science and Engineering, Communication Systems, Construction Engineering and Management, Embedded Systems, Power Electronics and Drives, Structural Engineering, Thermal Engineering, VLSI Design, and M.Tech in Information Technology, as well as M.B.A. and Ph.D. programs across engineering disciplines. The curriculum integrates challenge-based learning, value-added courses, and industry-oriented electives to foster skills like creativity, critical thinking, collaboration, and communication, aligning with 21st-century engineering needs.
In the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2025, KPRIET is placed in the 101-150 rank band for Engineering institutions in India. The institute maintains 65 research laboratories, promotes joint projects with industries and universities, and supports initiatives in entrepreneurship, sustainable development, and corporate social responsibility through centers like the Centre for Research and Development and the Innovation and Incubation Centre. However, in 2025, a Nature report noted the institute among those with a high number of retracted academic publications. Its campus features modern facilities including smart classrooms, a virtual tour-enabled environment, hostels, sports amenities, and green energy practices, contributing to a holistic educational experience. KPRIET also boasts strong placement outcomes, with graduates recruited by leading companies, and an active alumni network that aids in career development and institutional growth

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## Student Life
### Placements and Career Development
The KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) maintains a strong placement record, with over 90% of eligible students securing employment in recent years. According to the institute's NIRF 2025 submission, 902 undergraduate students out of 956 graduates from the 2020-2024 batch were placed, achieving a placement rate of approximately 94% for UG programs, while 49 out of 51 postgraduate students from the 2022-2024 batch were placed, yielding a 96% rate for PG programs. For the 2025 batch, as of June 2025, initial placements include 3 offers above 40 LPA, 5 above 30 LPA, and 18 above 20 LPA. The median salary package for UG placements stood at ₹5 LPA, and for PG at ₹4.5 LPA, reflecting steady employability in diverse sectors. The highest package offered during the 2024 drive reached ₹54 LPA, underscoring opportunities in high-growth industries.
Leading recruiters at KPRIET include IT giants such as TCS, Infosys, Cognizant, Wipro, HCL, and Zoho, alongside core engineering firms like Bosch and L&T Infotech, which target specific branches such as Civil Engineering. Over 165 companies participated in the 2024 placement drive, resulting in more than 850 offers extended to students across undergraduate and postgraduate levels.
The institute's Career Development Centre (CDC), led by Dr. B. Thamarai Kannan, plays a pivotal role in preparing students for professional success through comprehensive training initiatives. The CDC offers programs focused on soft skills, aptitude testing, coding proficiency, group discussions, mock interviews, and company-specific preparation, aiming to achieve 100% placement and enhance industry readiness. Internships are integrated into the curriculum as part of Employability Enhancement Courses, with mandatory industrial training required for third-year students to provide hands-on experience.
KPRIET's alumni network, established in 2016 through the Office of Alumni Relations, supports career development by organizing guest lectures, mentorship sessions, and endowments for student initiatives. The growing association fosters connections among graduates in major cities, contributing to ongoing industry collaborations and placement opportunities

# KPRIET Coimbatore: Admission 2026, Cutoff, Courses, Fees, Placements, Ranking
## About KPRIET Coimbatore
KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology Coimbatore often abbreviated as KPRIET Coimbatore, was established in 2009. The National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), and the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) have accredited the KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology Coimbatore, and it is also recognised by the University Grants Commission (UGC). The institute is also affiliated with Anna University, Chennai.

* **KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology**** Ranking: **The institute is** **ranked 101-150th rank band in the engineering category, as per the NIRF ranking 2025.
* **KPRIET courses **offered are BE/B.Tech, ME/M.Tech and PhD.
* The entrance exams accepted for **KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology admissions **are TNEA/GATE/TANCET/UGC/UGC-NET.
* **KPRIET fees**: for BE/B. Tech courses is Rs 1.10 lakhs; for ME/M.Tech courses is Rs 30,000.
* According to the NIRF 2025 data, the **KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology ****median package** offered is Rs 5 lakhs, and a total of 902 students got placed out of 956 graduating students.

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology courses are offered at undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral levels with about 12 B.E/B. Tech courses, 4 M.E courses, and 10 PhD courses. The duration of the said courses varies from 2 years to 6 years and the KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology's fee structure for these courses ranges from Rs 30,000 to Rs 1,10,000.

The entrance exams accepted for admissions by KPR Coimbatore are TNEA for UG admissions, and after that, the TNEA counselling round takes place, and GATE and TANCET for KPRIET admissions for PG courses, followed by the TANCA counselling round, and lastly, a written test conducted by Anna University for KPRIET Coimbatore doctoral admissions.

Based on the data of NIRF 2025, in the placements drive of 2024, around 902 UG students were placed, and Rs 5,00,000 per annum was the median salary package offered. KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology's infrastructure contains all the amenities, such as academic and research facilities, hostel blocks, sports complex, and other KPR Coimbatore facilities provided by the institution.

# Kpr Institute Of Engineering And Technology (KPRIET) Coimbatore
### Description
KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. It was established in 2009 by KPR Charities. The institute occupies 66 acres. KPRIET is an autonomous and unaided private institute. It is affiliated with Anna University, Chennai.

KPRIET offers 8 undergraduate programs, 4 postgraduate programs and 6 PhD programs. The institute has 12 academic departments with research centers in different engineering and pure science fields. It employs 260 faculty members and has around 3,500 students. There are also 280 administrative and support staff.

Facilities at KPRIET include smart classrooms, modern laboratories, high-speed wireless internet, auditoriums and seminar halls. The institute features a large centralized library, sports facilities and a gym.

Institute accepts TNEA scores for BE/BTech admissions and CEETA PG scores for MTech. KPRIET is approved by AICTE and accredited by NAAC with "A" grade. The courses also have NBA approval.

In 2023, KPRIET ranked 101 among 200 engineering colleges and 151 overall in India according to NIRF.

Placements- Over 450 students have secured placements in multinational companies. The highest CTC was Rs 20 LPA while the average CTC offered was Rs 4.2 LPA.

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is making rapid strides in providing industry ready students and exposing them to social issues and causes. KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology is ensconced in a 66.9 acres campus 12 km away from Coimbatore airport. The vision of the college is to become a premier institute of academic excellence by imparting technical, intellectual and professional skills to students for meeting the diverse needs of the industry, society, the nation and the world at large. To achieve such a vision, the college does not compromise on the quality of facilities provided. This extends not just to the physical infrastructure provided, but to curriculum, faculty and the extent and quality of exposure provided to the students. "œWe want to be a globally competitive institution," says Dr. K. Bommanna Raja, Principal, KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology. The college though began in 2009, is making rapid strides to provide high-quality engineers and students had already brought in numerous awards to the institute. The college is actively involved in research and development. The college has signed 18 Department level MoUs and eight institute level MoUs. Many of these MoUs are tied with consultancy projects worth Rs 54.7 lakh last year alone. A lot of research usually happens through the four Centres of Excellence the college has begun. "œWe have signed an MoU with a government agency and we are taking utmost care in developing that," reveals the Principal. Various MoUs have been signed with Greentech Solutions, Texas Instruments, and Reliance Jio Infocomm Limited among many others. Through these researches, students are able to take up projects that expose them to the industry experience. As they are involved in research, it is natural that their practical skills also make them more employable. The college has also taken up a project with Southern Region Railways in creating a database of the passengers and cataloging needs of the railway stations. All of these MoUs provide students exposure to the industry and experience. In the sphere of academics, there is a lot of focus given by the college. The learning environment provided is both student-centric and faculty-centric and academic audits are done through the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC). This makes the learning process work two ways with both student and faculty filling in the gaps in their respective spaces. With a faculty of over 190 the college maintains a student-faculty ratio of 1:15. The college delivers the curriculum through systematic planning, execution and monitoring mechanisms with a focus of 60 percent on academics and 40 percent on exposure. The curriculum includes guest lecturers, industrial visits and in-plant training. There are also student internships provided as well as content beyond the curriculum. This comes in the form of student enrichment programs and value added courses for every department. ICT tools are often used as part of the curriculum such as Webinars, TED videos, MOOC, NPTEL, and Video Lectures. There is also a well-established feedback mechanism to make the education program more effective. KPRIET understands the necessity of quality of faculty for providing quality technical knowledge to the students. The recruitment process is rigorous with four levels. Faculty are hired only once they are able to score 80 percent in all for levels of the recruitment process. Out of the 190 faculty, 24 are faculty from the industry providing an average industry experience of 13.7 years. Despite the fact that this college is affiliated, it has not stopped them from providing international exposure to foreign universities. Through video calls, Skype and conference calls, the students gain access to international faculty and courses. Visiting international faculty also provide courses and lectures to the students. These universities span from the USA to the U.K. Australia and South East Asian nations. The faculty is constantly put through numerous faculty development programs. These programs come through STTPs, seminars, conferences-international and national, faculty internships. With a Wi-Fi enabled campus with 50 Mbps internet connectivity and a computer lab with 797 computer students are able to work from anywhere within the campus. The college also has a well established Learning Resource Centre with 19,084 volumes, 7,879 titles of books and 130 journals. There is also a backlog of magazines and journals and the college is considered a local chapter of the National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning. The college also provides good hostel facilities for both boys and girls. There is also focus on sports and the college has provided such facilities as indoor-gym, multiple basketball courts and football fields, a track field and many others. This college also has an on-campus medical facility to deal with emergencies and a counseling center for the students

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. It was established in 2009 by KPR Charities. The institute occupies 66 acres. KPRIET is an autonomous and unaided private institute. It is affiliated with Anna University, Chennai.

KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology (KPRIET) is a private autonomous engineering college established in 2009 and located in the suburbs of Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India. Affiliated to Anna University, Chennai, and approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), it serves approximately 3,500 students with a faculty of 260 members, emphasizing innovation, research, and industry collaboration. The institute is accredited by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) with a CGPA of 3.08 on a four-point scale, earning an 'A' grade in its first cycle, valid until December 31, 2024. Several undergraduate programs, including those in Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Civil Engineering, are accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) for periods extending up to 2026.
KPRIET offers a range of undergraduate programs such as B.E. in Biomedical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Computer Science and Engineering (Cyber Security), Computer Science and Engineering (Data Science), Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning), Information Technology; postgraduate programs including M.E. in Applied Electronics, CAD/CAM, Computer Science and Engineering, Communication Systems, Construction Engineering and Management, Embedded Systems, Power Electronics and Drives, Structural Engineering, Thermal Engineering, VLSI Design, and M.Tech in Information Technology, as well as M.B.A. and Ph.D. programs across engineering disciplines. The curriculum integrates challenge-based learning, value-added courses, and industry-oriented electives to foster skills like creativity, critical thinking, collaboration, and communication, aligning with 21st-century engineering needs.
In the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2025, KPRIET is placed in the 101-150 rank band for Engineering institutions in India. The institute maintains 65 research laboratories, promotes joint projects with industries and universities, and supports initiatives in entrepreneurship, sustainable development, and corporate social responsibility through centers like the Centre for Research and Development and the Innovation and Incubation Centre. However, in 2025, a Nature report noted the institute among those with a high number of retracted academic publications. Its campus features modern facilities including smart classrooms, a virtual tour-enabled environment, hostels, sports amenities, and green energy practices, contributing to a holistic educational experience. KPRIET also boasts strong placement outcomes, with graduates recruited by leading companies, and an active alumni network that aids in career development and institutional growth

# KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology
## History
### Growth and Milestones
Since its establishment in 2009, KPR Institute of Engineering and Technology has undergone significant expansion in its academic offerings. Postgraduate programs were introduced to complement the initial undergraduate courses, allowing for advanced studies in engineering disciplines. The institute had expanded further to include PhD programs, establishing research centers in select departments under Anna University affiliation to foster doctoral-level research and innovation.
A pivotal milestone came in 2019 when the University Grants Commission (UGC) granted the institute full autonomous status, enabling greater flexibility in curriculum design, examination processes, and industry-aligned program development. The institute achieved NAAC accreditation with an A grade in 2015, reflecting its early commitment to quality education, followed by re-accreditation in 2019 with a CGPA of 3.08. Starting in 2017, the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) accredited select undergraduate programs such as Civil, Computer Science, Electronics and Communication, Electrical and Electronics, and Mechanical Engineering, with renewals extending up to 2026 for several of these. Additionally, KPRIET describes itself as a "21st Century Engineering College" focusing on modern pedagogy and employability skills.
The institute's student body has grown substantially from an initial enrollment of approximately 500 in its founding year to over 3,500 students by 2025, supported by a faculty strength that increased to 260 members, many with PhD qualifications. This expansion underscores the institute's rising appeal and capacity to deliver quality engineering education. In 2015, KPRIET established the KPR Incubation Centre (KIC) to nurture startups and entrepreneurship, providing mentorship, funding support, and facilities for student-led innovations in ICT and manufacturing sectors. Complementing this, the institute forged strategic partnerships with industries and international universities for curriculum enhancement, including joint research projects and skill development programs to align education with global standards. In 2023, NAAC extended the accreditation validity period, and in the NIRF 2025 rankings, KPRIET was placed in the 101-150 band for engineering institutions

# Female enrollment in master’s programmes hits all-time high
## Theme: Tech for whom?
### A mix of gender ratios
Certain schools and

master’s programmes at KTH

have for some time attracted a higher female-to-male ratio. In

Medical Engineering

, for example, the ratio has been generally even for some time, though women have represented a slightly larger portion of the enrollment since 2023.

Women have outnumbered men for at least the last six years in Architectural programme and

Sustainable Urban Planning

. In

Interactive Media Technology

, as well as

Molecular Techniques in Life Science

, female students have been in the majority since 2020, and their numbers have steadily increased to 62 and 65 percent respectively.

However, the numbers of international master’s students have more recently broken in favor of women in many programmes where male students have dominated. For example, in

Cybersecurity

over the last three years the percentage of women climbed from 17 to 38 percent. The

Industrial Management

programme records female enrollment of 48 percent – in contrast with only 17 percent three years earlier.

Women represent 44 percent of the 291 students from Sweden who enrolled in KTH master’s programmes. In KTH first cycle programmes, which are dominated by national students, the 33/67 female-to-male split has remained relatively unchanged over the last 10 years with little movement over time.

Other countries reflect a mix of international student enrollment. Women represent 40 percent of the 305 students who come to KTH from Chinese universities. While male students roughly double female students from universities in Germany, India, Canada and Italy.

*[Image]*

On the other hand, women dominate the more modest influx of students coming from universities in Spain, Iran, Poland, Mexico and Greece.

A more even split is seen in the combined 117 students that come from universities in Netherlands (51 percent women) and the U.S. (46 percent women)
